title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. subcanipes
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. subcanipes, also known as mountain hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with mountain hawkweed include reducing inflammation, wound healing, and potentially alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, mountain hawkweed can be found in infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall well-being.
